event: View of the facade of the Basilica of Santa Maria Maggiore
symbols: basilica facade — the enduring presence of the Church in the heart of Rome
bell tower — the call to worship rising above the city
description: A detailed black-and-white engraving showing the grand facade of a Roman basilica with a tall bell tower at the right. The facade features ornate decorative elements, columns, statues atop the roofline, and a central entrance. Adjacent buildings and ruins extend to the right. Small figures populate the plaza in front. Dark dramatic clouds fill the sky above. The style is precise and architectural with strong contrasts of light and shadow. Text is inscribed below the image.
theological_description: The Basilica of Santa Maria Maggiore is one of the four major papal basilicas in Rome, traditionally believed to have been founded after the Virgin Mary appeared in a vision directing Pope Liberius to build a church where snow would fall in August. As the largest church in Rome dedicated to the Virgin Mary, it embodies the Church's devotion to the Theotokos and houses the relic of the manger from Bethlehem. The architectural grandeur captured in this engraving reflects the theological conviction that sacred architecture should manifest the glory of God and the dignity of worship. The basilica form itself — derived from Roman civic buildings — symbolizes the Church's transformation of worldly structures into spaces of divine encounter.

scripture_refs.reason: All generations will call me blessed, the Mighty One has done great things — the Marian devotion the basilica embodies
How lovely is your dwelling place, O Lord — the beauty of God's house
Solomon's dedication of the Temple — the archetype of sacred architecture dedicated to God's presence

_meta.title: View of the facade of the Basilica of St. Maria Maggiore
